description: Phase 4 - CI/CD Pipeline with GitHub Actions: Create GitHub Actions workflows for automated deployment to AWS. Includes: build and test on PR, deploy frontend to S3/CloudFront on merge to main, deploy backend Lambdas, and run CDK diff on PRs for infrastructure review.
steps: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml: On PR - install deps, typecheck, test, build (validates the PR is green)

Create .github/workflows/deploy-frontend.yml: On push to main - build the Vue app, sync dist/ to S3, invalidate CloudFront cache. Uses OIDC for AWS auth (no long-lived secrets). Environment: production.
Create .github/workflows/deploy-backend.yml: On push to main (when backend/ changes) - install backend deps, bundle Lambdas with esbuild, run cdk deploy for backend stack. Uses OIDC for AWS auth.
Create .github/workflows/cdk-diff.yml: On PR (when infra/ changes) - run cdk diff and post the output as a PR comment for infrastructure review
Add deployment configuration to infra/ CDK: GitHub OIDC provider IAM role for GitHub Actions (with least-privilege policy scoped to the specific resources)
Update README.md with CI/CD documentation: required GitHub secrets/variables (AWS_ACCOUNT_ID, AWS_REGION, CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID, S3_BUCKET_NAME), OIDC trust relationship setup
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow runs on PRs and validates build + tests
Deploy frontend workflow syncs to S3 and invalidates CloudFront on main push
Deploy backend workflow bundles and deploys Lambdas only when backend/ changes
CDK diff workflow posts infrastructure changes as PR comment
All workflows use OIDC for AWS authentication (no stored AWS access keys)
verification: npx yaml-lint .github/workflows/*.yml 2>/dev/null || python3 -c "import yaml; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in __import__('glob').glob('.github/workflows/*.yml')]" (validate YAML syntax)
findings: All 4 workflow files pass YAML validation. Infra typecheck passes including new github-oidc-stack.ts. Frontend typecheck passes. Only pre-existing test failure in questionStore.test.ts (documented in task context). Used Node 22 in CI workflows as specified.
